name: @grpc/grpc-js
what_it_does: Pure JavaScript gRPC implementation for Node.js (replacement for the deprecated native grpc package). @grpc/grpc-js implements the gRPC protocol over HTTP/2 with Protocol Buffer support, streaming (unary, server-streaming, client-streaming, bidirectional), deadlines, cancellation, and TLS. The standard gRPC client and server library for Node.js.
use_cases: Build high-performance agent-to-agent RPC services with strongly-typed Protocol Buffer contracts over HTTP/2
Implement bidirectional streaming between agent orchestrators and workers for real-time progress streaming and control commands
Create server-streaming agent APIs where the server pushes multiple responses (results, status updates) to a single client request
Connect Node.js agents to existing gRPC backends (Go, Java, Python services) using generated protobuf clients
Implement gRPC interceptors for cross-cutting agent concerns — auth injection, retry logic, logging, tracing across all RPC calls
not_for: Browser clients — gRPC-Web requires a proxy (Envoy) or different library; use @grpc/grpc-js for Node.js only
Simple REST-style APIs — gRPC has significant setup overhead (protobuf schemas, code generation) vs HTTP REST for simple use cases
Teams without protobuf toolchain — gRPC requires protoc and grpc_tools_node_protoc for code generation; adds build complexity
best_when: Building microservices where strong typing, streaming, and performance matter, and your team is comfortable with protobuf schema-first development.
avoid_when: Simple APIs, browser clients, or teams without protobuf toolchain experience — REST/HTTP with OpenAPI is significantly lower friction.

auth.notes: gRPC supports TLS (channel credentials) and call credentials (metadata-based token auth). mTLS supported for mutual authentication. Auth is passed via CallCredentials metadata interceptors.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — limited by network and server capacity. HTTP/2 multiplexing provides high concurrency on single connections.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: gRPC errors include status codes (NOT_FOUND, UNAVAILABLE, DEADLINE_EXCEEDED) with details. Error messages from server are propagated to client. gRPC status codes are well-defined but the mapping from application errors to status codes requires careful design.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: gRPC has deadline/retry semantics. Retries require server-side idempotency — gRPC doesn't enforce this automatically. Set waitForReady for connection retries.
known_agent_gotchas: Protobuf code generation requires protoc + grpc_tools_node_protoc_ts packages — the build toolchain setup is non-trivial compared to REST; plan for proto compilation in CI
gRPC channel reuse is critical — creating a new gRPC channel per request is expensive; create channels once and reuse with a connection pool
Deadlines propagate across services in gRPC — set deadline on the outermost call; inner services inherit and respect it for end-to-end timeout enforcement
Unhandled server errors that don't call callback() or end() leave client streaming RPCs hanging indefinitely — always call callback even in error paths
gRPC metadata (headers) must be set before the first message is sent — adding metadata to an already-started stream is silently ignored
@grpc/grpc-js has different default behavior from the native grpc package for keepalive — configure keepalive parameters explicitly for long-lived agent connections

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS strongly recommended and well-supported. mTLS provides mutual authentication. gRPC interceptors enable consistent auth enforcement. Pure JS implementation avoids native addon security surface.
